description: A designed DHS 'National Immigrant Heritage Month' spotlight card in the series template: deep navy-blue background with a faint large American-flag watermark and the white bold headline 'DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month' at top. Centered is a rectangular photo of a bearded man in a dark CBP/officer uniform wearing a campaign-style trooper hat, arms crossed, standing in an indoor hall in front of a stone memorial wall engraved with names. Below a large white quotation mark, the white testimonial reads: "[Immigrant Heritage Month] reminds me where I came from and the struggles and sacrifices that my parents had to endure so that I have a better future. Being an immigrant can be very challenging since you have to adjust to different cultures and people, but it also makes you resilient, humble, and able to overcome any difficult situation." A blue rounded banner at bottom-left reads: 'Alexander M. / CBP, Customs and Border Protection Officer / 1st generation from Albania.' The circular DHS seal is at bottom-right. This is pro-immigrant-heritage workforce-spotlight messaging from the prior administration; target audience is the public and DHS workforce, emphasizing resilience and gratitude.
notable_text: DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month; "[Immigrant Heritage Month] reminds me where I came from and the struggles and sacrifices that my parents had to endure..."; Alexander M., CBP, Customs and Border Protection Officer, 1st generation from Albania
